extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by versus against each entity: I counted the Company’s 3 criminal, 3 tax and 1 statutory/regulatory proceedings, plus the Promoter’s 1 criminal proceeding; I excluded all “By the Company,” “By the Promoter,” and other “By” rows, and excluded the Promoter’s 12 tax proceedings from the company-group tax count because they are personal to the Promoter. The quantified amount against the Company is ₹4.66 million; no subsidiary row or aggregate company/subsidiary total is disclosed, so this is the only quantified group-against component, converted to ₹0.466 crore. Contingent liabilities use the stated Total of ₹6.95 million as at June 30, 2023, converted to ₹0.695 crore, rather than summing the line items. The disclosed percentages relate to loan portfolios, AUM, or other business/segment measures, not company-wide top-five or top-ten customer revenue as a percentage of revenue from operations or total income for a full fiscal year, so both concentration fields are null. The litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million; conversion is ₹1 million = ₹0.1 crore.
